croix_maya.title: Mayan Cross
croix_maya.subtitle: Classical K'iche' tradition · Bolontiku confirmed only for G9
croix_maya.position_centre_text: Your birth nawal is the fundamental essence of your being. It reveals your natural gifts and the energetic color that tints your entire existence. It is your deep identity in the sacred K'iche' calendar.
croix_maya.position_east_text: The energy of the East represents your conception, the original impulse that set you in motion. It is the energy of dawn and the first breath — the courage that brought you into being. It reveals your deep origins and the initial force of your existence.
croix_maya.position_west_text: The energy of the West represents your mission in this life, the path you came to fulfill. It is the energy of sunset and transformation. It reveals what you naturally tend toward — your deep destination.
croix_maya.position_north_text: The energy of the North is your inner guide, the wisdom of the ancestors that orients you. It is the cold breath carrying memory and clarity. This energy guides your choices and illuminates your life path.
croix_maya.position_south_text: The energy of the South is your natural support, the warmth that nourishes and strengthens you. It is the energy of the body, abundance, and community. It represents your allies and the resources you can rely on.
croix_maya.position_trecena_text: The trecena is the 13-day period to which your birth kin belongs. It always begins at tone 1 and colors the entire cycle. The nawal that opens your trecena is your period master — it orients the fundamental energy in which you emerged. In K'iche' tradition, the 8th day of each trecena (Wajxaqib') is the most auspicious for ceremonies: 8 is the divine number of renewal and beginning.
croix_maya.position_bearer_text: The year bearer governs the annual cycle for the entire community. It colors the collective energy and influences the context in which events unfold. The birth year reveals in which cosmic breath you arrived.
croix_maya.position_lord_text: The Bolontiku — the Nine Lords of the Night — form a cycle of 9 nights that runs continuously alongside the Tzolk'in. The night of your birth was placed under the reign of one of them: a subterranean, nocturnal, and inner energy that complements your solar kin. Where the day nawal reveals who you are in the light — your gifts, your mission, your social dynamics —, the Lord of the Night reveals who you are in the shadow: your inner world, your dreams, your invisible processes, your relationship with the unconscious and the hidden forces of life. Together, they form the double thread of your being.

how_it_works.title: The Tzolk'in: The Spiritual Calendar of the Maya
how_it_works.cycles_title: Astronomical cycles and their relationship with the Tzolk'in
how_it_works.resonances_title: Numerical resonances with other ancient traditions
how_it_works.intro_p1: The Tzolk'in, also called the "count of days" or "sacred calendar," is one of the calendars of the Maya civilization. Originating from Mesoamerica, this calendrical system was developed over 2,500 years ago. Its use has continued uninterrupted to this day in certain Maya communities of Guatemala and Mexico.
how_it_works.intro_p2: Unlike our Gregorian calendar, which follows solar cycles, the Tzolk'in is based on a 260-day cycle, resulting from the combination of two simultaneous cycles: a cycle of 13 numbers and a cycle of 20 glyphs (called "nawals"). Each day is thus identified by a number from 1 to 13 paired with one of the 20 glyphs, creating 260 unique combinations. This cycle corresponds approximately to the period of human gestation and is also linked to agricultural and astronomical cycles, central to Maya civilization.
how_it_works.cycles_text: The Maya possessed remarkably precise astronomical knowledge, which is reflected in the structure of the Tzolk'in. The 260-day cycle presents several significant astronomical correlations: it corresponds approximately to nine synodic lunar cycles (about 29.5 days each) as well as the visibility period of Venus. Moreover, Maya observers had noted that the Sun's zenith passages at the latitude of Copán (approximately 14.8° North) occurred at 260-day intervals. The 20-day cycle, linked to the Maya vigesimal (base 20) system, also reflects the cycle of the Pleiades, visible for about 260 days per year at these latitudes. The cycle of 13, in turn, could correspond to the number of "heavens" or cosmic levels in Maya cosmology. The Maya had calculated with stunning precision the synodic cycles of the visible planets, solar and lunar eclipses, and even the precession of the equinoxes, a cycle of approximately 26,000 years. The Tzolk'in was part of a broader calendrical system, including the Haab (a 365-day solar calendar), forming the "Long Count," which tracked periods spanning several millennia.
how_it_works.resonances_text: Remarkably, the numbers 13 and 20, at the heart of the Maya Tzolk'in, appear with similar significance in other culturally distant traditions. In the Celtic tradition, the lunar calendar comprised 13 months of 28 days, corresponding to the 13 lunar cycles of a solar year. The number 20 appears in their vigesimal counting system, and certain Celtic stone circles feature arrangements based on these numbers. The Celtic Ogham calendar, for example, has 20 main signs, reminiscent of the 20 glyphs of the Tzolk'in. Among the ancient Egyptians, the cycle of Sirius (the brightest star in the sky) was particularly important, and its visibility period was approximately 260 days per year. Their calendar featured 13 phases corresponding to the Nile floods, and their numerical system attributed particular significance to the number 20 as a marker of a complete period. In the Hopi tradition of North America, we find a prophetic system based on 13 major cycles, and their cosmology divides the world into 20 fundamental aspects. This numerical convergence between cultures, apparently without direct contact, raises fascinating questions about the potential universality of certain astronomical observations or about fundamental mathematical intuitions common to humanity.
how_it_works.glyphs_text: The 20 glyphs of the Tzolk'in represent fundamental spiritual forces or energies that influence each day according to Maya cosmogony. Each glyph possesses its own characteristics, symbolism, and spiritual significance. They are represented by complex visual symbols evoking natural elements, animals, or abstract concepts: Imix (Crocodile), Ik (Wind), Akbal (Night), Kan (Seed), Chicchan (Serpent), Cimi (Death), Manik (Deer), Lamat (Star), Muluc (Water), Oc (Dog), Chuen (Monkey), Eb (Grass), Ben (Reed), Ix (Jaguar), Men (Eagle), Cib (Vulture), Caban (Earth), Etznab (Flint), Cauac (Storm), and Ahau (Lord/Sun). These glyphs determine the fundamental nature of each day and are considered portals for communicating with different dimensions of existence and specific deities.
how_it_works.numbers_text: In the Tzolk'in, the 13 numbers are not mere quantitative indicators but possess deep qualitative and spiritual significance. Each number from 1 to 13 represents a particular intention, being, or energy direction: 1 symbolizes unity and initiation, 2 duality and polarity, 3 activation and movement, 4 stability and measure, 5 harmony and center, 6 balance and rhythm, 7 reflection and meditation, 8 harmony and justice, 9 patience and perseverance, 10 manifestation and accomplishment, 11 dissolution and liberation, 12 understanding and stability, and 13 ascension and transcendence. According to Maya tradition, each number modulates the energy of the glyph to which it is associated, creating a unique dynamic for each day of the calendar. These numbers are also linked to the thirteen major joints of the human body, establishing a connection between the macrocosm and the microcosm.
how_it_works.calendar_text: The ◀ ▶ arrows navigate day by day. Click on the date → free entry (birth, event…). ↺ returns to today. Every element is clickable — glyph, number, trecena — and opens a description page.
how_it_works.corn_text: Below the calendar, the colored frame indicates the K'iche' corn family of the day — Red (East), White (North), Black (West) or Yellow (South). Click on it to access the family page: Popol Vuh, element, the 5 K'iche' nawals and associated ceremonies.
how_it_works.cross_text: Personal map from your date of birth — five dimensions: Center birth · East conception · West mission · North guide · South support. Plus the Lord of the Night G1–G9. Each position is clickable. Accessible from "Important day for" or from the ✛ button in Profiles.
how_it_works.notes_text: Choose an emotion from Plutchik's wheel and write freely. Automatic saving. Also possible for a past day (date, time and location confirmed, city pre-filled by GPS).
how_it_works.notes_emotions:
Joy · Serenity · Ecstasy
Sadness · Pensiveness · Grief
Fear · Apprehension · Terror
Anger · Annoyance · Rage
Surprise · Distraction · Amazement
Trust · Acceptance · Admiration
Disgust · Loathing · Boredom
Anticipation · Interest · Vigilance
how_it_works.saved_text: Complete, filterable history. Download as CSV, TXT or PDF. CSV reimport available. Automatic annual backup — keep it safe.
how_it_works.saved_other_day: Save for another day: navigate to the desired day in the calendar, open Notes — a date confirmation will be requested before entering time and location.
how_it_works.saved_edit: Editing from Saved: each note is fully editable — text, time, location. The 📍 button activates GPS to automatically fill your location if permission is granted.
how_it_works.settings_contacts: Manage your contacts for the Mayan Cross. Save as many names and birth dates as you wish, choose a color. For each contact: Edit, Delete, and the ✛ button directly opens their Mayan Cross.
how_it_works.settings_pin: This is also where you set up an optional PIN code to protect your notes.
how_it_works.quote: "Know thyself and thou shalt know the Universe and the Gods"

analysis.no_notes: No notes to export. Add notes before ordering an analysis.
analysis.email_body:
Hello,

I would like to order my annual Tzolkin analysis.
You will find my logbook attached.

We remind you that the analysis of your data (tzolkin, transits, psyche, astronomy) will cost 50 euros. It will be processed as soon as you have paid the amount via the link received in response to this email.

We hope you enjoy our application.

Best regards,
The Galaad Motokiyo Ferran team

P.S. Check your spam folder. The payment link should normally arrive instantly after you send this email.
analysis.web_fallback: Please attach the file {filename} that was just downloaded.
analysis.section_description: Analysis of your TzolkInSight year from your .csv (not worthwhile without enough data — minimum 100 to 150 entries over a year): glyph recurrence, tones, trecenas, transits, cycles, Jungian psyche…
analysis.confirm_item_csv: Send your Tzolk'in journal (CSV) to our team
analysis.confirm_item_payment: Receive a payment link of €50 by email
analysis.confirm_item_analysis: Receive your personalised analysis after payment
analysis.confirm_checkbox: I understand that this submission triggers a paid order of €50 and that I will receive my analysis by email after payment.
